justification: All pass criteria met: Both models described accurately with structural differences (single order vs composite basket with sub-orders). AC2 behavioral divergence explicitly named (all-or-nothing cancellation vs per-vendor cancellation). Escalation routed to Coach K/product owner for domain model decision, not to Shaq or MJ. No hybrid model produced — explicitly states the contradiction is 'not resolvable through synthesis.' MJ's missing architecture noted as additional blocker with recommendation to block on domain resolution first.

observations.text: Exceptionally clear articulation of the three-axis contradiction: order model shape, partial fulfillment validity, and vendor failure blast radius
Correctly refuses to produce a handoff brief, citing unresolved fundamental conflict
AC2 divergence explicitly named: 'one vendor failure cancels the entire order' vs 'one vendor failure cancels only that vendor's sub-order'
Decision routed to Coach K/product owner for arbitration, not to Shaq or MJ
MJ's missing architecture correctly identified as a separate blocker, with recommendation to sequence it after domain resolution
Provides three clear options including a recommended arbitration session rather than picking a side
Low confidence score (20) appropriately reflects inability to resolve the conflict without authority input
Identifies an additional unaddressed concern (payment model implications) showing depth of analysis
